tool.name: KAT Filter Kmer
tool.description: filters k-mers to those within and outside defined bounds
tool.command:
## Link input file with appropriate extension #if $input.ext in ('fastq.gz', 'fastqsanger.gz'): #set $ext = '.fastq.gz' #elif $input.ext in ('fasta.gz',): #set $ext = '.fasta.gz' #elif $input.ext in ('fastq', 'fastqsanger'): #set $ext = '.fastq' #elif $input.ext == 'jellyfish': #set $ext = '.jf' #else: #set $ext = '.fasta' #end if cp '$input' 'input${ext}' &&
kat filter kmer -o kat-filter-kmer -t \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} -c '$filter_options.low_count' -d '$filter_options.high_count' -g '$filter_options.low_gc' -h '$filter_options.high_gc' $filter_options.invert $filter_options.separate $kmer_options.non_canonical -m '$kmer_options.mer_len' -H '$kmer_options.hash_size' 'input${ext}' && mv kat-filter-kmer-in* kat-filter-kmer-in.jf #if $filter_options.separate: && mv kat-filter-kmer-out* kat-filter-kmer-out.jf #end if

param.name: input
param.label: Input
param.help: Input FASTA/FASTQ file (gzip accepted) or a pre-counted Jellyfish hash file to filter k-mers from

param.label: Low count threshold
param.help: K-mers with coverage below this value are excluded from the selected set

param.label: High count threshold
param.help: K-mers with coverage above this value are excluded from the selected set

param.label: Low GC count threshold
param.help: K-mers with a GC count below this value are excluded from the selected set

param.label: High GC count threshold
param.help: K-mers with a GC count above this value are excluded from the selected set

param.label: Invert selection
param.help: Take k-mers outside the defined region as the selected content, rather than those inside

param.label: Separate into two sets
param.help: Partition k-mers into two output hashes: those inside the defined region and those outside. Works in combination with Invert

tool.help:
**KAT Filter Kmer - K-mer Filtering by Coverage and GC Bounds**
KAT (K-mer Analysis Toolkit) Filter Kmer produces Jellyfish k-mer hashes containing k-mers that fall within (or outside) user-defined GC count and k-mer coverage bounds.
-----
**What it does**
Given an input FASTA/FASTQ file or pre-counted Jellyfish hash, this tool filters k-mers according to two criteria applied simultaneously:
- **K-mer coverage** - retain only k-mers whose count falls between the low and high count thresholds - **GC count** - retain only k-mers whose GC nucleotide count falls between the low and high GC thresholds
This is particularly useful for isolating k-mers attributable to contamination or for contamination removal, typically after identifying suspect regions using the KAT GCP plot.
The selection can be **inverted** to instead capture k-mers *outside* the defined bounds, and the **separate** option can be used to produce both sets simultaneously as distinct output hashes.
-----
**Inputs**
- **Input**: A FASTA or FASTQ file (gzip compression accepted), or a pre-counted Jellyfish hash file (`.jf`).
-----
**Outputs**
- **Selected k-mers hash**: A Jellyfish hash containing the k-mers that passed the filter (or failed, if *Invert* is enabled). - **Non-selected k-mers hash** *(optional)*: A Jellyfish hash of the complementary set of k-mers. Only produced when *Separate into two sets* is enabled.
-----
**Notes**
- GC thresholds are expressed as **counts** (number of G/C bases in the k-mer), not percentages. For a k-mer length of 27, a GC count of 1–100 effectively covers all possible GC values. - Use *Invert* alone to flip which set is written to the primary output. Use *Separate* to write both sets regardless of inversion. - The default **hash size** (100 million) suits most datasets; adjust downward to reduce memory use on smaller inputs or upward for large, high-coverage datasets. - If the input is already a Jellyfish hash, k-mer length and hash size settings are ignored.
